choice: NextAuth v5 (Auth.js) with a Credentials provider validating email+password (zod-checked) against the users table with bcryptjs; JWT session strategy carrying user id + householdId. Split config: edge-safe auth.config.ts (no DB, providers:[], authorized callback gates all routes) used by Next 16 proxy.ts; full auth.ts (Credentials + DB) used by the API route and server components. Closed membership seeded via scripts/seed.ts (db:seed; SEED_EMAIL/PASSWORD/NAME/HOUSEHOLD), idempotent on email; no public signup. The (app) layout also re-checks the session (defense in depth).
rationale: [build-originated] Decision c1c6e5f9 fixed Auth.js+credentials+closed-membership but not the session strategy or edge-middleware structure. JWT (not DB sessions) avoids an adapter for a credentials-only closed app; the split config keeps DB/bcrypt out of the edge runtime (Next 16 renamed middleware->proxy).
